content.preview: === PIR.ec: 22 lemma statement(s) (signatures only — proofs are NOT shown) === These are every lemma declared in the current file. Use them to plan which lemmas you can apply/rewrite/bridge with; `lookup_symbol` for the exact declaration of any one before using it. L20 [lemma, top_level] sxor_cons: lemma sxor_cons s i : sxor s (i :: s) i. L23 [lemma, top_level] sxor2_cons: lemma sxor2_cons (s s':int list) (i j:int): sxor2 s s' i => sxor2 (j::s) (j::s') i. L66 [lemma, top_level] PIR_correct: lemma PIR_correct &m i0 : 0 <= i0 < N => Pr [PIR.main(i0) @ &m : res = a i0] = 1%r. L71 [equiv, top_level] PIR_secure1: equiv PIR_secure1: PIR.main ~ PIR.main : true ==> ={PIR.s}. L81 [equiv, top_level] PIR_secure2: equiv PIR_secure2: PIR.main ~ PIR.main : true ==> ={PIR.s'}. L91 [lemma, top_level] PIR_secuity_s_byequiv: lemma PIR_secuity_s_byequiv i1 i2 &m1 &m2 x: Pr[PIR.main(i1) @ &m1 : PIR.s = x] = Pr[PIR.main(i2) @ &m2 : PIR.s = x]. L95 [lemma, top_level] PIR_secuity_s'_byequiv: lemma PIR_secuity_s'_byequiv i1 i2 &m1 &m2 x: Pr[PIR.main(i1) @ &m1 : PIR.s' = x] = Pr[PIR.main(i2) @ &m2 : PIR.s' = x]. moves.title: Read-only tactic probe
moves.applicability: This tactic-shaped option should be checked with a read-only probe before committing it.
moves.effect: This is a read-only probe; it asks EasyCrypt what would happen without changing the committed proof state.
moves.derivation: read-only probe surfaced from proof-state analysis
moves.guarantee: read-only probe shape; proof state unchanged; probe then decide; reversible

moves.tactic_shape: Probabilistic VC plan: Pr loss-accounting via have-chain, rewrite Pr/mu rules, real-order smt
moves.applicability: Use this as route-selection context; it is not a tactic to run as-is.
